findings.title: CWE-330: Weak PRNG (`Random`) in web handler at line 1257
findings.description: `new Random()` at L1257 in a web-facing method. Weak randomness in web endpoints enables session prediction and CSRF token bypass.
findings.suggestion: Use `java.security.SecureRandom` for web-facing random values.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak random number generator in `setChatAdministratorCustomTitle()`
findings.description: java.util.Random or Math.random() used — not cryptographically secure.
findings.suggestion: Use java.security.SecureRandom for security-sensitive randomness.

findings.title: CWE-328: Weak cryptographic algorithm (SHA-1) at line 29
findings.description: `getInstance("RSA/ECB/OAEPWithSHA-1AndMGF1Padding")` at L29. SHA-1 is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks.
findings.suggestion: Use SHA-256 or stronger. For passwords, use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak PRNG (`Random`) at line 79
findings.description: `new Random()` used at L79. `java.util.Random` is not cryptographically secure. An attacker can predict its output.
findings.suggestion: Use `java.security.SecureRandom` for security-sensitive values (tokens, session IDs, crypto keys). `new SecureRandom()` is the drop-in replacement.
